perturbation.original_text: Licensed Equifax Materials (excluding the Utility Software Programs) to such Group member's customers solely for each such customer's internal business purposes to the extent related to the Certegy Business. All sublicensing by Certegy and other members of the Certegy Group to any one of their customers shall be pursuant to written agreements with such customer, executed before or at the time of furnishing each copy of the Licensed Equifax Materials (excluding the Utility Software Programs) to such customer, and which provide at a minimum that such customer: (i) receives only a personal, non-transferable and nonexclusive right to use such copy of the Licensed Equifax Materials (excluding the Utility Software Programs);
perturbation.changed_text: Licensed Equifax Materials (excluding the Utility Software Programs) to such Group member's customers for any purposes and without limits. All sublicensing by Certegy and other members of the Certegy Group to any one of their customers shall be pursuant to any form of agreements with such customer, executed at any moment, and which provide at a minimum that such customer: (i) receives the rights to use such copy of the Licensed Equifax Materials (excluding the Utility Software Programs);
perturbation.explanation: The original text limits the sublicense rights of the Licensed Equifax Materials only for internal business purposes. The modification removes these restrictions making it applicable for all purposes. This is an in-text contradiction to the first statment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) The sublicense rights granted to Certegy pursuant to Section 4.1(a) include the right for Certegy to grant sublicenses to the Licensed Equifax Materials (excluding the Utility Software Programs) to the members of the Certegy Group, which sublicenses may include the right to further sublicense such Licensed Equifax Materials (excluding the Utility Software Programs) to such Group member's customers solely for each such customer's internal business purposes to the extent related to the Certegy Business. All sublicensing by Certegy and other members of the Certegy Group to any one of their customers shall be pursuant to written agreements with such customer, executed before or at the time of furnishing each copy of the Licensed Equifax Materials (excluding the Utility Software Programs) to such customer, and which provide at a minimum that such customer: (i) receives only a personal, non-transferable and nonexclusive right to use such copy of the Licensed Equifax Materials (excluding the Utility Software Programs);
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts sublicensing to "internal business purposes", while the changed text removes these restrictions allowing for "any purposes and without limits". This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that sublicenses may only be granted for "internal business purposes". This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the changed text broadens the scope of sublicensing rights beyond what is permitted in the contradicted section, potentially leading to disputes over the permissible uses of the licensed materials.

perturbation.original_text: Unless Exhibit K provides otherwise, Equifax, or the respective Designated Equifax Member, shall own all the modifications and improvements to, and the Equifax Enhancements and/or Derivative Works made from, the Licensed Certegy Materials developed by any member of the Equifax Group, or by any party other than a member of the Certegy Group at the expense of the Equifax Group. Certegy hereby assigns, and shall cause each member of the Certegy Group to assign, to Equifax, or the respective Designated Equifax Member, all right, title and interest it may hold in and to such modifications, improvements, Equifax Enhancements and Derivative Works.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless Exhibit K provides otherwise, Certegy, or the respective Designated Certegy Member, shall own all the modifications and improvements to, and the Equifax Enhancements and/or Derivative Works made from, the Licensed Certegy Materials developed by any member of the Equifax Group, or by any party other than a member of the Certegy Group at the expense of the Equifax Group. Certegy hereby assigns, and shall cause each member of the Certegy Group to assign, to Equifax, or the respective Designated Equifax Member, all right, title and interest it may hold in and to such modifications, improvements, Equifax Enhancements and Derivative Works.
perturbation.explanation: In the original contract, it states that Equifax owns all the modifications and improvements. The modified version says Certegy owns the modifications, which contradicts the definition provided.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Unless Exhibit K provides otherwise, Equifax, or the respective Designated Equifax Member, shall own all the modifications and improvements to, and the Equifax Enhancements and/or Derivative Works made from, the Licensed Certegy Materials developed by any member of the Equifax Group, or by any party other than a member of the Certegy Group at the expense of the Equifax Group. Certegy hereby assigns, and shall cause each member of the Certegy Group to assign, to Equifax, or the respective Designated Equifax Member, all right, title and interest it may hold in and to such modifications, improvements, Equifax Enhancements and Derivative Works.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that Equifax owns the modifications and improvements, which aligns with the contradicted text. However, the changed text states that Certegy owns the modifications, directly contradicting the provision in the contradicted text. This creates a clear and irreconcilable conflict regarding ownership, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability. The change violates the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition as the ownership clause is now placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: Following the Closing Date, Equifax shall, and shall cause each member of the Equifax Group to, cooperate in an orderly transfer of the Transferred Equifax Assets and the Transferred Equifax Third Party Agreements to Certegy or the respective Designated Certegy Member. From time to time, at Certegy's request and without further consideration, Equifax shall, and shall cause each member of the Equifax Group, as applicable, to execute, acknowledge and deliver such documents, instruments or assurances and take such other action as Certegy may reasonably request to more effectively assign, convey and transfer any of the Transferred Equifax Assets and the Transferred Equifax Third Party Agreements.
perturbation.changed_text: Following the Closing Date, Equifax may choose, depending of their interest on supporting Certegy, to cooperate in an orderly transfer of the Transferred Equifax Assets and the Transferred Equifax Third Party Agreements to Certegy or the respective Designated Certegy Member. From time to time, at Certegy's request and with extra consideration, Equifax may choose to, and shall cause each member of the Equifax Group, as applicable, to execute, acknowledge and deliver such documents, instruments or assurances and take such other action as Certegy may reasonably request to more effectively assign, convey and transfer any of the Transferred Equifax Assets and the Transferred Equifax Third Party Agreements.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, Equifax *shall* cooperate with Certegy in asset transfers *without further consideration*. This change makes cooperation optional ('Equifax may choose') and requires 'extra consideration,' directly contradicting the original obligation and its financial terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Following the Closing Date, Equifax shall, and shall cause each member of the Equifax Group to, cooperate in an orderly transfer of the Transferred Equifax Assets and the Transferred Equifax Third Party Agreements to Certegy or the respective Designated Certegy Member. From time to time, at Certegy's request and without further consideration, Equifax shall, and shall cause each member of the Equifax Group, as applicable, to execute, acknowledge and deliver such documents, instruments or assurances and take such other action as Certegy may reasonably request to more effectively assign, convey and transfer any of the Transferred Equifax Assets and the Transferred Equifax Third Party Agreements.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text states Equifax 'shall' cooperate 'without further consideration,' while the changed text states Equifax 'may choose' to cooperate 'with extra consideration.' This introduces a direct contradiction by making cooperation optional and requiring additional payment, which is against the original and contradicted text.
